close_reason: Added PKCS#11 v2.40+ version requirement to module-level rustdoc
description:
Location: secretx-pkcs11/src/lib.rs:1-3 (top-of-crate doc) and README.md line 5.

What is unclear: README claims "compatible with any PKCS#11 v2.40-compliant token". The crate doc top says "URI: secretx:pkcs11:slot/label[?lib=path]" but doesn't restate the version requirement. The actual hard requirements:

  - PKCS#11 v2.40+ for CKA_PUBLIC_KEY_INFO read in public_key_der (line 538). v2.20 / v2.30 do not define this attribute.
  - PKCS#11 v2.20+ for CKM_SHA256_RSA_PKCS_PSS (line 501).
  - PKCS#11 v2.20+ for CKM_ECDSA_SHA256 (line 489).
  - OS_LOCKING_OK semantics (v2.01+).

What would make it clear: in the rustdoc top-of-crate, add "# Requirements" section listing PKCS#11 ≥ v2.40 with rationale (CKA_PUBLIC_KEY_INFO). Also note that `lib` may be a v2.40 wrapper around an older driver (some vendors do this); in that case, signing works but public_key_der may fail.

Cross-references: see crate-secretsx-nde2.11 (public_key_der fallback path).
title: PKCS#11 version requirement only stated in README, not in rustdoc top-of-crate
